model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Duke is the pick because the underlying quality gap is still enormous: both KenPom and Torvik rate Duke No. 1 overall with an elite top-5 offense and the nation’s best defense, while TCU profiles as a solid but clearly lower-ceiling top-50 team. Duke’s main concern is availability (Caleb Foster remains out with a foot fracture), but the Blue Devils’ depth and defensive ceiling should keep TCU’s offense from sustaining efficient scoring for 40 minutes. TCU’s upset path is real if it turns the game into a physical grind and wins the 3-point/turnover variance, but Duke’s baseline efficiency margin makes it the much more likely winner.

reasoningStep.title: East R32: Duke vs TCU
reasoningStep.summary: Duke advances on overwhelming two-way efficiency despite a notable guard injury, with TCU needing variance to threaten.
evidence: Ratings: Duke is #1 on KenPom (AdjEM 39.17; AdjO #4; AdjD 88.7) and #1 on Torvik (AdjO rank #4; AdjD rank #1).
Ratings: TCU is ~#43 on KenPom (AdjEM 17.62; AdjO rank #81; AdjD 97.8) and ~#48 on Torvik (AdjO rank #83; AdjD rank #30).
Availability: ESPN reported Duke starting guard Caleb Foster is out indefinitely with a foot fracture; AP also noted Duke was without Foster for the ACC Tournament.
Matchup: TCU’s defense is good enough to keep it competitive if it slows pace and wins variance margins, but Duke’s elite defense plus high-end offense provides multiple paths to separation.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.

The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
